title: Work-Life Balance

vocabulary.meaning: a plan that shows when things will happen
time spent working beyond normal hours
time spent relaxing and recovering energy
worry or pressure caused by difficult situations
kept in a healthy and fair proportion

questions: Do you work to live or live to work? Do you find your job stressful?
How does your job compare with 10 years ago? Is it more stressful or less stressful today?
What do you do to make sure you have a good work-life balance?
Do you have a good way of dealing with stress?
Have you ever been burnt out, or know anyone has become burnt out?
Is admitting you are burnt out a taboo in your company (or country)?
Are some personality types more susceptible to burnout?
'Modern employees are constantly under pressure to do more work in less time.' Do you agree?
Does your company try to make sure employees have a good work-life balance?
How would you like your company to address this issue?
How important are: a) exercise, b) diet and c) family-life to a good work-life balance?
